mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in payroll employment above 150k signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative when PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.05 in 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in both consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive when both CPIAUCSL and HOUST decline, or the interaction effect's t-statistic < 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105 signals improved supply chain efficiency and corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative when INDPRO exceeds 105, or the signal's Rank IC < 0.03 in 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 2.5% signals monetary tightening that increases equity discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive during FEDFUNDS > 2.5% periods, or the signal's t-statistic < 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) below 2% growth signals recession risks that depress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive when GDPC1 declines below 2%, or the signal's Rank IC < 0.05 in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 5% and decline in real sales (RSAFS) suggests weak labor demand and consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when both UNRATE > 5% and RSAFS declines, or the interaction effect's t-statistic < 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 107 combined with stable inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2.0%) signals strong supply-side growth without price pressures, driving positive SPX returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative when INDPRO > 107 and CPIAUCSL < 2.0%, or the interaction effect's Rank IC < 0.03.
